name: Coriander Chops
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown coriander seeds in a frying pan over medium heat until fragrant.
2. Allow the seeds to cool, then crush or grind them with black pepper and set aside.
3. Combine chopped vegetables, rosemary, and bay leaves in a separate bowl.
4. Coat both sides of pork chops with crushed coriander and pepper mixture.
5. Heat oil in a heavy frying pan over medium heat.
6. Add the pork chops to the pan and brown on both sides until golden.
7. Remove the pork chops and set aside.
8. In the same pan, add vegetables, white wine, and pork broth. Bring to a simmer.
9. Cook for 12-15 minutes or until the pork is cooked through.
10. Thicken the sauce by mixing cornstarch with white wine in a separate bowl.
11. Add the thickened sauce back into the pan and adjust seasoning if needed.
12. Serve the sauce with the pork chops, garnished with fresh cilantro or parsley if desired.
